paper.title: BrainPilot: Automating Brain Discovery with Agentic Research

checks: The downloaded object is a readable PDF 1.7 document; pdfinfo reports 47 pages and 21819949 bytes.
The layout-preserving extraction is non-empty and readable from the title and abstract through the methods, evaluation, conclusion, references, and complete appendices ending on manuscript page 47.
The extraction preserves the paper's four BrainPilotBench-v0 task specifications, including required paths, artifact schemas, scoring constraints, and private-evaluation boundaries.
The immutable arXiv source archive is readable and contains 28 members, including main.tex, references.bib, and the paper figures.
The arXiv API summary contains no withdrawal or retraction notice.
warnings: pdftotext/pdfinfo emitted repeated 'Unterminated string' and 'End of file inside dictionary' syntax warnings near PDF object 1924, but extraction completed, all 47 page boundaries are present, and sampled beginning, benchmark appendix, and final page are readable.

The PDF page header displays 2026-07-18 while the immutable arXiv API record says v1 was published and last updated on 2026-07-16; preserve the API timestamp and note the manuscript-header discrepancy in review.

official_releases.timing_boundary: Both pinned repository commits were authored after the arXiv v1 publication timestamp. They are acquisition-time official releases and must not be assumed to be the exact paper-time implementation without further history analysis.
acquisition_findings: The BrainPilot archive contains 1059 files and 74 SKILL.md files, including eight meta-skills; the paper's 72-methodology-unit denominator requires review-time reconciliation.
The BrainPilot archive contains Graph of Trace implementation and tests plus skill creation and verification machinery.

The BrainPilot archive's knowledge source, chunk, model, and vector-store payload directories are placeholders; its documentation explicitly says the public package does not ship a hosted knowledge base.

The separate BrainPilotBench archive contains four released v0 task packages: bciciv-2a, neuro-rsc-place-cell, sleep-edf, and tops-fmri, with prompts, data locks, setup, checks, and solution witnesses.

Archive inventory found rendered evaluation figures but no raw paper trial trajectories, complete score records, cost receipts, or provider receipts. This is an acquisition observation, not a completed deep audit.

acquisition_scope: Downloaded the real immutable arXiv v1 PDF and source archive, extracted layout-preserving full text, verified readable coverage through the final appendix page, and pinned both paper-linked official repositories at acquisition-time commits. This is source acquisition and extraction verification, not a deep paper/code review, benchmark replay, result reproduction, endorsement, or evidence of domain-expertise transfer, scientific validity, cost advantage, professional capability, or readiness.
review_handoff: Use the immutable v1 PDF/text/source and both pinned official archives. Reconcile the paper's 72 skills with 74 released SKILL.md files; distinguish AI-generated skill notices from expert verification; inspect Graph of Trace and Auditor evidence views; compare the four paper appendix tasks to the four released packages; and search release/history for raw ALE/BrainPilotBench/case-study runs and cost receipts. Preserve the post-v1 release timing boundary and all unsupported claim ceilings.
local_storage_note: The PDF, arXiv source archive, and repository archives are local evidence. The extracted full text, metadata, paper-index entry, inventory, and this provenance record are intended for version control; re-fetch large artifacts from immutable URLs and verify SHA-256 when absent locally.
